questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: Let each of the circles $\begin{aligned} & S_1=x^2+y^2+4 y-1=0 \\ & S_2=x^2+y^2+6 x+y+8=0 \\ & S_3=x^2+y^2-4 x-4 y-37=0 \end{aligned}$ touches the other two. Let $P_1, P_2, P_3$ be the point of contact of $S_1$ and $S_2, S_2$ and $S_3, S_3$ and $S_1$ respectively. Let $T$ be the point of concurrence of the tangents at $P_1, P_2, P_3$ to the circles. $C_1, C_2$, $\mathrm{C}_3$ are the centres of $\mathrm{S}_1, \mathrm{~S}_2, \mathrm{~S}_3$ respectivelyQuestion: The area of the quadrilateral $\mathrm{TP}_2 \mathrm{C}_3 \mathrm{P}_3$ is
questions.solution: Shown in figure $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{P}_1(-2,-1) \\ & \mathrm{P}_2(-4,-1) \\ & \mathrm{P}_3(-1,-4) \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: A circle $C$ whose radius is 1 unit, touches the $x$-axis at point $A$. The centre $Q$ of $C$ lies in first quadrant. The tangent from origin $O$ to the circle touches it at $T$ and a point $P$ lies on it such that $\Delta O A P$ is a right angled triangle at $A$ and its perimeter is 8 unitsQuestion: The length of $Q P$ is
questions.solution: Given $\mathrm{QT}=\mathrm{QA}=1$ Let $P Q=x$, then $P T=\sqrt{x^2-1}$ Then $\triangle$ TQP and $\triangle$ APO are similar triangles Then $O T=O A=\frac{x+1}{\sqrt{x^2-1}}$ $\Rightarrow 1+\mathrm{x}+\frac{2(\mathrm{x}+1)}{\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^2-1}}+\sqrt{\mathrm{x}^2-1}=8$ $\Rightarrow x=\frac{5}{3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: Let each of the circles $\begin{aligned} & S_1=x^2+y^2+4 y-1=0 \\ & S_2=x^2+y^2+6 x+y+8=0 \\ & S_3=x^2+y^2-4 x-4 y-37=0 \end{aligned}$ touches the other two. Let $P_1, P_2, P_3$ be the point of contact of $S_1$ and $S_2, S_2$ and $S_3, S_3$ and $S_1$ respectively. Let $T$ be the point of concurrence of the tangents at $P_1, P_2, P_3$ to the circles. $C_1, C_2$, $\mathrm{C}_3$ are the centres of $\mathrm{S}_1, \mathrm{~S}_2, \mathrm{~S}_3$ respectivelyQuestion: $P_2$ and $P_3$ are reflections of each other in the line
questions.solution: Shown in figure $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{P}_1(-2,-1) \\ & \mathrm{P}_2(-4,-1) \\ & \mathrm{P}_3(-1,-4) \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: A circle $C$ whose radius is 1 unit, touches the $x$-axis at point $A$. The centre $Q$ of $C$ lies in first quadrant. The tangent from origin $O$ to the circle touches it at $T$ and a point $P$ lies on it such that $\Delta O A P$ is a right angled triangle at $A$ and its perimeter is 8 unitsQuestion: Equation of circle C is
questions.solution:
From above, $O A=2$ and $A Q=1$
$\Rightarrow$ coordinate of $Q \equiv(2,1)$
$\Rightarrow$ equation is $(x-2)^2+(y-1)^2=1$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A rectangle $P Q R S$ is inscribed in a circle $C$ with centre $O$ and a diameter along the line $2 y-4 x=9$. $P$ and $Q$ are the points $(-8,-2)$ and $(4,-2)$ respectively. Let a circle $C_1$ be drawn taken RS as the diameter. On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question: Area of rectangle PQRS is -
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{O}(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ be the centre of the circle C $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 2 \mathrm{k}-4 \mathrm{~h}=9 \quad \ldots . . . \text { (i) } \\ & \text { Also } \mathrm{OP}=\mathrm{OQ}=\text { Radius of the circle } \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{OP}^2=\mathrm{OQ}^2 \quad \text { or } \quad \mathrm{h}+8= \pm(\mathrm{h}-4) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}+8=-\mathrm{h}+4 \quad \Rightarrow \mathrm{~h}=-2 \end{aligned}$
Putting in (i), $\mathrm{k}=\frac{1}{2}$ $\begin{array}{ll} \therefore & \text { Radius }=\mathrm{OP}=\frac{13}{2} \\ \therefore & P R=13 \end{array}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { In } \triangle \mathrm{PQR}, \mathrm{PR}^2=\mathrm{PQ}^2+\mathrm{QR}^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 169=144+\mathrm{QR}^2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{QR}=5 \end{aligned}$ Also co-ordinates of $\mathrm{R} \& \mathrm{~S}$ are respectively $(4,3) \&(-8,3)$ $\text { Area of Rectangle PQRS }=12 \times 5=60 \text { sq.units }$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A rectangle $P Q R S$ is inscribed in a circle $C$ with centre $O$ and a diameter along the line $2 y-4 x=9$. $P$ and $Q$ are the points $(-8,-2)$ and $(4,-2)$ respectively. Let a circle $C_1$ be drawn taken RS as the diameter. On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question: If $m_1 \& m_2$ are the finite slopes of tangent to circle $C_1$ from $P \& Q$ respectively, then the value of $\left|\mathrm{m}_1\right|+\left|\mathrm{m}_2\right|$ is -
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{O}(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ be the centre of the circle C $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 2 \mathrm{k}-4 \mathrm{~h}=9 \quad \ldots . . . \text { (i) } \\ & \text { Also } \mathrm{OP}=\mathrm{OQ}=\text { Radius of the circle } \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{OP}^2=\mathrm{OQ}^2 \quad \text { or } \quad \mathrm{h}+8= \pm(\mathrm{h}-4) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}+8=-\mathrm{h}+4 \quad \Rightarrow \mathrm{~h}=-2 \end{aligned}$
Putting in (i), $\mathrm{k}=\frac{1}{2}$ $\begin{array}{ll} \therefore & \text { Radius }=\mathrm{OP}=\frac{13}{2} \\ \therefore & P R=13 \end{array}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { In } \triangle \mathrm{PQR}, \mathrm{PR}^2=\mathrm{PQ}^2+\mathrm{QR}^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 169=144+\mathrm{QR}^2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{QR}=5 \end{aligned}$ Also co-ordinates of $\mathrm{R} \& \mathrm{~S}$ are respectively $(4,3) \&(-8,3)$ Now $C_1:(x-4)(x+8)+(y-3)(y-3)=0$ or $x^2+y^2+4 x-6 y-23=0$ centre $(-2,3) \quad \&$ Radius $=6$ Equation of tangent through P to $\mathrm{C}_1$ is $y+2=m_1(x+8)$ $\Rightarrow\left|\frac{6 \mathrm{~m}_1-5}{\sqrt{1+\mathrm{m}_1^2}}\right|=6 \Rightarrow \mathrm{~m}_1=-\frac{11}{60}$ Similarly, equation of tangent through $Q$ to $C_1$ is $\mathrm{y}+2=\mathrm{m}_2(\mathrm{x}-4)$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow\left|\frac{-6 \mathrm{~m}_2-5}{\sqrt{1+\mathrm{m}_2^2}}\right|=6 \Rightarrow \mathrm{~m}_2=\frac{11}{60} \\ & \therefore \quad\left|\mathrm{~m}_1\right|+\left|\mathrm{m}_2\right|=\frac{11}{30}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A rectangle $P Q R S$ is inscribed in a circle $C$ with centre $O$ and a diameter along the line $2 y-4 x=9$. $P$ and $Q$ are the points $(-8,-2)$ and $(4,-2)$ respectively. Let a circle $C_1$ be drawn taken RS as the diameter. On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question: A line through $(7,5)$ at maximum distance from point $O$ will have slope equal to -
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{O}(\mathrm{h}, \mathrm{k})$ be the centre of the circle C $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 2 \mathrm{k}-4 \mathrm{~h}=9 \quad \ldots . . . \text { (i) } \\ & \text { Also } \mathrm{OP}=\mathrm{OQ}=\text { Radius of the circle } \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{OP}^2=\mathrm{OQ}^2 \quad \text { or } \quad \mathrm{h}+8= \pm(\mathrm{h}-4) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{h}+8=-\mathrm{h}+4 \quad \Rightarrow \mathrm{~h}=-2 \end{aligned}$
Putting in (i), $\mathrm{k}=\frac{1}{2}$ $\begin{array}{ll} \therefore & \text { Radius }=\mathrm{OP}=\frac{13}{2} \\ \therefore & P R=13 \end{array}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { In } \triangle \mathrm{PQR}, \mathrm{PR}^2=\mathrm{PQ}^2+\mathrm{QR}^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 169=144+\mathrm{QR}^2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{QR}=5 \end{aligned}$ Also co-ordinates of $\mathrm{R} \& \mathrm{~S}$ are respectively $(4,3) \&(-8,3)$ The line through $(7,5)$ at maximum distance from centre $O$ of circle $C$ will be the line perpendicular to line joining $(7,5) \&$ point $O$. $\Rightarrow$ slope of required line $=\frac{-1}{\frac{5-1 / 2}{7+2}}=-2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the following curves. $\begin{aligned} & C_1: y^2=4 x \\ & C_2: y^2(a+b x)=(a+c x)^2, a, b, c \in I^{+}\end{aligned}$ where $C_2$ is the locus of the family of circles which passes through focus of $C_1$ and cuts $C_1$ orthogonally. On the basis of above information, answer the following questions:Question: The area bounded by the curve $C_2 \&$ the ordinates $x=-\frac{1}{3}$ and $x=1$ -
questions.solution:
Let the circle be $x^2+y^2+2 g x+2 f y+c=0$ If passes through $(1,0)$ $\therefore \quad 1+2 g+c=0$ ...(i) Let $\left(t^2, 2 t\right)$ be the point of intersection of two curves $\therefore \quad$ equation of tangent is $\mathrm{yt}=\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{t}^2$ ...(ii) $\because \quad$ Circle \& parabola cuts orthogonaly $\therefore \quad$ (ii) passes through the centre of the circle $\therefore \quad-f t=t^2-g$ $f t=g-t^2$ ...(iii) $\left(\mathrm{t}^2, 2 \mathrm{t}\right)$ also satisfies equation of circle $\therefore \quad t^4+4 t^2+2 \mathrm{gt}^2+4 f t+c=0$ Putting the value of $\mathrm{c} \& f \mathrm{t}$ in above equation $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{t}^4+4 \mathrm{t}^2+2 \mathrm{gt}^2+4 \mathrm{~g}-4 \mathrm{t}^2-1-2 \mathrm{~g}=0 \\ & \mathrm{t}^4+2 \mathrm{gt}^2+2 \mathrm{~g}-1=0 \\ & \left(\mathrm{t}^4-1\right)+2 \mathrm{~g}\left(\mathrm{t}^2+1\right)=0 \\ & \mathrm{t}^2=1-2 \mathrm{~g} \end{aligned}$ squaring equation (iii) we get $\begin{aligned} & f^2 t^2=\left(g-t^2\right)^2 \\ & \Rightarrow f^2(1-2 g)=(3 g-1)^2\left\{\text { Putting the value of } t^2\right\} \\ & \text { replacing }-g \text { by x } \&-f \text { by y we get, } \\ & y^2(1+2 x)=(1+3 x)^2\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & y^2=\frac{(1+3 x)^2}{(1+2 x)} \Rightarrow y= \pm \frac{(1+3 x)}{\sqrt{1+2 x}} \\ & \text { Consider } y=\frac{1+3 x}{\sqrt{1+2 x}} \\ & y^{\prime}=\frac{3 x+2}{(1+2 x)^{3 / 2}}\gt0 \quad \forall x\gt-\frac{1}{2} \end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{A}=2 \int_{-1 / 3}^1 \frac{1+3 \mathrm{x}}{\sqrt{1+2 \mathrm{x}}} \mathrm{dx} \\ & \text { Put } 1+2 \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{t}^2 \\ & \mathrm{dx}=\mathrm{tdt}\end{aligned}$ Solving we get $\mathrm{A}=\frac{20 \sqrt{3}}{9}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the following curves. $\begin{aligned} & C_1: y^2=4 x \\ & C_2: y^2(a+b x)=(a+c x)^2, a, b, c \in I^{+}\end{aligned}$ where $C_2$ is the locus of the family of circles which passes through focus of $C_1$ and cuts $C_1$ orthogonally. On the basis of above information, answer the following questions:Question: If the tangents to the curve $C_1$ at $P(1,2)$ and $Q(4,-4)$ meets at point $R$, if the area of triangle $P Q R$ is $A$ then $[\mathrm{A}]$, (where [.] is greatest integer function), is -
questions.solution:
Let the circle be
$x^2+y^2+2 g x+2 f y+c=0$
If passes through $(1,0)$
$\therefore \quad 1+2 g+c=0$   ...(i)
Let $\left(t^2, 2 t\right)$ be the point of intersection of two curves
$\therefore \quad$ equation of tangent is
$\mathrm{yt}=\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{t}^2$   ...(ii)
$\because \quad$ Circle \& parabola cuts orthogonaly
$\therefore \quad$ (ii) passes through the centre of the circle
$\therefore \quad-f t=t^2-g$
$f t=g-t^2$   ...(iii)
$\left(\mathrm{t}^2, 2 \mathrm{t}\right)$ also satisfies equation of circle
$\therefore \quad t^4+4 t^2+2 \mathrm{gt}^2+4 f t+c=0$

Putting the value of $\mathrm{c} \& f \mathrm{t}$ in above equation
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{t}^4+4 \mathrm{t}^2+2 \mathrm{gt}^2+4 \mathrm{~g}-4 \mathrm{t}^2-1-2 \mathrm{~g}=0 \\
& \mathrm{t}^4+2 \mathrm{gt}^2+2 \mathrm{~g}-1=0 \\
& \left(\mathrm{t}^4-1\right)+2 \mathrm{~g}\left(\mathrm{t}^2+1\right)=0 \\
& \mathrm{t}^2=1-2 \mathrm{~g}
\end{aligned}$
squaring equation (iii) we get
$\begin{aligned} & f^2 t^2=\left(g-t^2\right)^2 \\ & \Rightarrow f^2(1-2 g)=(3 g-1)^2\left\{\text { Putting the value of } t^2\right\} \\ & \text { replacing }-g \text { by x } \&-f \text { by y we get, } \\ & y^2(1+2 x)=(1+3 x)^2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Let $C_1: x^2+y^2=r^2$ and $C_2:(x-p)^2+(y-q)^2=r^2$ be 2 circles with radius $r(r>0)$ and have $n$ points of intersection, $\left(x_i, y_i\right)$ for $i \in\{1,2, \ldots \ldots, n\}$. If $C_1$ and $C_2$ are orthogonal at all points of intersection, then:
Question: As we move the centre of $C_2$ along $p+b q=0$ for some constant $b \neq 0$, then $\frac{d r}{d q}$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& (x-p)^2+(y-q)^2=x^2+y^2=r^2 \\
\therefore \quad & p^2+q^2=2 p x+2 q y ...(1)
    \end{aligned}
$$

For the circle to intersect orthogonally
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \left.\left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right|^{C_1} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}\right|^{C_2}=-1 \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \left(\frac{-x}{y}\right)\left(\frac{-(x-p)}{y-q}\right)=-1 \\
& x^2-p x=q y-y^2 \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-p x-qy=0 ...(2)
\end{aligned}
$$

From eqns. (1) and (2) $\Rightarrow p^2+q^2=2\left(x^2+y^2\right)=2 r^2...(3)$
Now, $q=a \Rightarrow$ differential $2 p+0=4 r \frac{d r}{d p}$
$\therefore \quad \frac{d r}{d p}=\frac{p}{2 r}$
Now, $\quad p+b q=0 \Rightarrow$ put $p=-b q$ in eqn. (3)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \left(b^2+1\right) q^2=2 r^2 \\
& \left(b^2+1\right) 2 q=4 r \frac{d r}{d q}
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore \quad \frac{d r}{d q}=\frac{\left(b^2+1\right) q}{2 r}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Let $C_1: x^2+y^2=r^2$ and $C_2:(x-p)^2+(y-q)^2=r^2$ be 2 circles with radius $r(r>0)$ and have $n$ points of intersection, $\left(x_i, y_i\right)$ for $i \in\{1,2, \ldots \ldots, n\}$. If $C_1$ and $C_2$ are orthogonal at all points of intersection, then:
Question: As we move the centre of $C_2$ along the curve $q=a$ for some constant $a \neq 0$, then $\frac{d r}{d p}$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& (x-p)^2+(y-q)^2=x^2+y^2=r^2 \\
\therefore \quad & p^2+q^2=2 p x+2 q y ...(1)
    \end{aligned}
$$

For the circle to intersect orthogonally
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \left.\left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right|^{C_1} \cdot \frac{d y}{d x}\right|^{C_2}=-1 \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \left(\frac{-x}{y}\right)\left(\frac{-(x-p)}{y-q}\right)=-1 \\
& x^2-p x=q y-y^2 \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-p x-qy=0 ...(2)
\end{aligned}
$$

From eqns. (1) and (2) $\Rightarrow p^2+q^2=2\left(x^2+y^2\right)=2 r^2...(3)$
Now, $q=a \Rightarrow$ differential $2 p+0=4 r \frac{d r}{d p}$
$\therefore \quad \frac{d r}{d p}=\frac{p}{2 r}$
Now, $\quad p+b q=0 \Rightarrow$ put $p=-b q$ in eqn. (3)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \left(b^2+1\right) q^2=2 r^2 \\
& \left(b^2+1\right) 2 q=4 r \frac{d r}{d q}
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore \quad \frac{d r}{d q}=\frac{\left(b^2+1\right) q}{2 r}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Given two fixed points A and B in a plane and a positive real number k not equal to unity. Introduce a Given two fixed points $A$ and $B$ in a pla choosing mid-point of $A B$ as origin and the positive direction of $x$ axis is chosen from A to B . The locus of points M for which the equation $\frac{M A}{M B}=k$ holds is a circle $C_k$.Question: An arbitrary circle passes through points $A$ and $B$ intersects the circle $C_k$ at an angle
questions.solution:
Let $A \equiv(-a, 0)$ and $B \equiv(a, 0)$
Locus of point $M$ is $\frac{(x+a)^2+y^2}{(x-a)^2+y^2}=k^2\left(\right.$ As $\left.\frac{M A}{M B}=k\right)$
$\Rightarrow C_k \equiv x^2+y^2+2 a x \frac{\left(1+k^2\right)}{\left(1-k^2\right)}+a^2=0$
If $C_{k_1}$ and $C_{k_2}$ are symmetric about $y$-axis
$\Rightarrow\left(\frac{1+k_1^2}{1-k_1^2}\right)+\left(\frac{1+k_2^2}{1-k_2^2}\right)=0$
$\Rightarrow k_1 k_2=1$
Let any circle passes through $A$ and $B$ Its centre will always lie on $y$-axis
$\Rightarrow x^2+(y-\beta)^2=a^2+\beta^2$
Angle between the circles is given by
$\theta=\pi-\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{r_1^2+r_2^2-d^2}{2 r_1 r_2}\right)$, where $r_1$ and $r_2$ are radii of circles and $d$ is distance between their centres
$\Rightarrow \theta=\pi-\cos ^{-1}\left[\frac{a^2\left(\frac{1+k^2}{1-k^2}\right)^2-a^2+a^2+\beta^2-\left(a^2\left(\frac{1+k^2}{1-k^2}\right)^2+\beta^2\right)}{2 \times\left(a^2+\beta^2\right)\left[a^2\left(\frac{1+k^2}{1-k^2}\right)^2-a^2\right]}\right]$
$\Rightarrow \alpha=\pi-\cos ^{-1}(0)=\pi-\frac{\pi}{2}=\frac{\pi}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Given two fixed points A and B in a plane and a positive real number k not equal to unity. Introduce a Given two fixed points $A$ and $B$ in a pla choosing mid-point of $A B$ as origin and the positive direction of $x$ axis is chosen from A to B . The locus of points M for which the equation $\frac{M A}{M B}=k$ holds is a circle $C_k$.Question: If the circles $C_{k_1}$ and $C_{k_2}\left(0 \lt k_1 \neq 1,0 \lt k_2 \neq 1\right)$, are symmetric to each other with respect to the perpendicular bisector of segment $A B$, then
questions.solution:
Let $A \equiv(-a, 0)$ and $B \equiv(a, 0)$
Locus of point $M$ is $\frac{(x+a)^2+y^2}{(x-a)^2+y^2}=k^2\left(\right.$ As $\left.\frac{M A}{M B}=k\right)$
$\Rightarrow C_k \equiv x^2+y^2+2 a x \frac{\left(1+k^2\right)}{\left(1-k^2\right)}+a^2=0$
If $C_{k_1}$ and $C_{k_2}$ are symmetric about $y$-axis
$\Rightarrow\left(\frac{1+k_1^2}{1-k_1^2}\right)+\left(\frac{1+k_2^2}{1-k_2^2}\right)=0$
$\Rightarrow k_1 k_2=1$
Let any circle passes through $A$ and $B$ Its centre will always lie on $y$-axis
$\Rightarrow x^2+(y-\beta)^2=a^2+\beta^2$
Angle between the circles is given by
$\theta=\pi-\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{r_1^2+r_2^2-d^2}{2 r_1 r_2}\right)$, where $r_1$ and $r_2$ are radii of circles and $d$ is distance between their centres
$\Rightarrow \theta=\pi-\cos ^{-1}\left[\frac{a^2\left(\frac{1+k^2}{1-k^2}\right)^2-a^2+a^2+\beta^2-\left(a^2\left(\frac{1+k^2}{1-k^2}\right)^2+\beta^2\right)}{2 \times\left(a^2+\beta^2\right)\left[a^2\left(\frac{1+k^2}{1-k^2}\right)^2-a^2\right]}\right]$
$\Rightarrow \alpha=\pi-\cos ^{-1}(0)=\pi-\frac{\pi}{2}=\frac{\pi}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let ABC be a triangle equation of whose sides are $\mathrm{AB} \equiv \mathrm{x}+2 \mathrm{y}=3, \mathrm{AC} \equiv 2 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=3$, and $\mathrm{BC} \equiv \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=4$. Circles $\mathrm{S}_1, \mathrm{~S}_2$ and $\mathrm{S}_3$ are drawn taking $\mathrm{AB}, \mathrm{AC}$ and BC as diameter respectively. Now $\mathrm{L}_{12}=0, \mathrm{~L}_{31}=0$ and $\mathrm{L}_{23}=0$ are the radical axis of $\mathrm{S}_1$ and $\mathrm{S}_2, \mathrm{~S}_3$ and $\mathrm{S}_1$, and $\mathrm{S}_2$ and $\mathrm{S}_3$ respectively. These radical axis intersects the sides $\mathrm{BC}, \mathrm{AC}$ and AB at points $\mathrm{D}, \mathrm{E}$ and F respectively. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: Radical centre of $S_1, S_2$ and $S_3$ will be -
questions.solution:
Coordinates of $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}$ and C are $\mathrm{A}(1,1)$, $\mathrm{B}(5,-1), \mathrm{C}(-1,5)$. Now equation of the circles taking $\mathrm{AB}, \mathrm{AC}$ and BC as diameter respectively will be
$\begin{aligned} S_1 & \equiv(x-1)(x-5)+(y-1)(y+1)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-6 x+4=0\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} \mathrm{S}_2 \equiv & (\mathrm{x}-1)(\mathrm{x}+1)+(\mathrm{y}-1)(\mathrm{y}-5)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-6 y+4=0 \\ \mathrm{~S}_3 \equiv & \equiv(x-5)(x+1)+(y+1)(y-5)=0 \\ & \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-4 x-4 y-10=0\end{aligned}$

Equation of radical axis of $S_2$ and $S_3$ will be
$\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{L}_{23} & \equiv 4 \mathrm{x}-2 \mathrm{y}+14=0 \\
& \Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{y}+7=0
\end{aligned}$
clearly radical centre will be the point of intersection of $L_{12}=0 \& L_{23}=0$ i.e., $(-7,-7)$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Let ABC be a triangle equation of whose sides are $\mathrm{AB} \equiv \mathrm{x}+2 \mathrm{y}=3, \mathrm{AC} \equiv 2 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=3$, and $\mathrm{BC} \equiv \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}=4$. Circles $\mathrm{S}_1, \mathrm{~S}_2$ and $\mathrm{S}_3$ are drawn taking $\mathrm{AB}, \mathrm{AC}$ and BC as diameter respectively. Now $\mathrm{L}_{12}=0, \mathrm{~L}_{31}=0$ and $\mathrm{L}_{23}=0$ are the radical axis of $\mathrm{S}_1$ and $\mathrm{S}_2, \mathrm{~S}_3$ and $\mathrm{S}_1$, and $\mathrm{S}_2$ and $\mathrm{S}_3$ respectively. These radical axis intersects the sides $\mathrm{BC}, \mathrm{AC}$ and AB at points $\mathrm{D}, \mathrm{E}$ and F respectively. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: Equation of $L_{12}=0$ is -
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the two points $\mathrm{A}(3,7)$ and $\mathrm{B}(6,5)$. Family of circles S is drawn passing through these two points. A circle C is also given whose equation is $\mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-4 \mathrm{x}-6 \mathrm{y}-3=0$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: The common chord of the circle $C$ and family of circle $S$ will always pass through the point -
questions.solution:
Equation of family of circles passing through A \& B is given by
$\begin{aligned}
& (x-3)(x-6)+(y-7)(y-5)+\lambda(2 x+3 y-27)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x^2+y^2+(2 \lambda-9) x+(3 \lambda-12) y+53-27 \lambda=0
\end{aligned}$

Equation of the common chord of (i) and C is
$\begin{aligned}
& (2 \lambda-9+4) x+(3 \lambda-12+6) y+53-27 \lambda+3=0 \\
& (5 x+6 y-56)-\lambda(2 x+3 y-27)=0
\end{aligned}$
which passes through the point of intersection of $5 x+6 y-56=0$ and $2 x+3 y-27=0$ i.e. $(2,23 / 3)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the two points $\mathrm{A}(3,7)$ and $\mathrm{B}(6,5)$. Family of circles S is drawn passing through these two points. A circle C is also given whose equation is $\mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-4 \mathrm{x}-6 \mathrm{y}-3=0$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: Equation of the member of the family S which bisects the circumference of C is -
questions.solution:
Centre $(2,3)$ will lie on the common chord so $-28+14 \lambda=0 \Rightarrow \lambda=2$
so equation of required circle will be
$x^2+y^2-5 x-6 y-1=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the two points $\mathrm{A}(3,7)$ and $\mathrm{B}(6,5)$. Family of circles S is drawn passing through these two points. A circle C is also given whose equation is $\mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-4 \mathrm{x}-6 \mathrm{y}-3=0$. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question: If O is origin and P is the centre of C , then difference of the squares of the length of the tangents from $A$ and $B$ to the circle $C$ is equal to -
questions.solution:
Difference of the square of the length of the tangents from A and B to the circle C is
$\begin{aligned}
& |(9+49-12-42-3)-(36+25-24-30-3)|=3 \\
& \mathrm{AB}^2=13, \mathrm{OP}^2=13, \mathrm{AP}^2=17, \mathrm{BP}^2=20
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the family of circles $x^2+y^2-2 x-2 \lambda y-8=0$ passing through two fixed points $A$ and $B$. Also $S=0$ is a circle of this family, the tangent to which at $A$ and $B$ intersect on the line $x+2 y+5=0$.Question: The distance between the points $A$ and $B$, is
questions.solution:
(i) We have $\left(x^2+y^2-2 x-8\right)-2 \lambda y=0$, which is the form of $S+\lambda L=0$ $\therefore$ On solving $S=0$ and $L=0$ where $S \equiv x^2+y^2-2 x-8=0$ and $L \equiv y=0$ Put $y=0 \Rightarrow x^2-2 x-8=0(x-4)(x+2)=0 \quad x=-2$ or 4 $\therefore \quad A \equiv(4,0) ; B \equiv(-2,0)$ $\Rightarrow$ Distance between $A$ and $B=6$ (ii) Equation of $A B$ which is chord of contact with respect to $P\left(x_1, y_1\right)$ is $\mathrm{xx}_1+\mathrm{yy}_1-1\left(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x}_1\right)-\lambda\left(\mathrm{y}+\mathrm{y}_1\right)-8=0$ $\Rightarrow x\left(x_1-1\right)+y\left(y_1-\lambda\right)-\left(x_1+\lambda y_1+8\right)=0$ $\ldots(1)$
Also equation of $A B$ is $x$-axis i.e $0 \cdot x+1 \cdot y+0 \cdot c=0$ $\ldots(2)$ $\therefore$ On comparing (1) \& (2), we get $\frac{\mathrm{x}_1-1}{0}=\frac{\mathrm{y}_1-\lambda}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{x}_1+\lambda \mathrm{y}_1+8}{0}=k \text { say }$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}_1-1=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{x}_1=1 ; \mathrm{y}_1=\lambda+\mathrm{k}\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { and } x_1+\lambda y_1+8=0 \\ & \Rightarrow 9+\lambda y_1=0\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{y}_1=-\frac{9}{\lambda}$ Also $\mathrm{x}_1+2 \mathrm{y}_1+5=0 \Rightarrow 1-\frac{18}{\lambda}+5=0$ $\Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{18}{6} \Rightarrow \lambda=3$ $\therefore \quad$ Required equation of circle $S=0$ is $x^2+y^2-2 x-6 y-8=0$ Now radius of circle $S=0$ is $r=\sqrt{1+9+8}=3 \sqrt{2}$ Hence area of equilateral triangle inscribed in a circle of radius ' $r$ ' $=\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{4} r^2=\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{4} \cdot 18$ $=\frac{27 \sqrt{3}}{2}$ (iii) If the circle $x^2+y^2-10 x+2 y+c=0$ is orthogonal to $S \equiv x^2+y^2-2 x-6 y-8=0$, so by applying condition of orthogonality, we get $2[(-5)(-1)+(1)(-3)]=c-8 \Rightarrow 4=c-8$ Hence $c=12$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the family of circles $x^2+y^2-2 x-2 \lambda y-8=0$ passing through two fixed points $A$ and $B$. Also $S=0$ is a circle of this family, the tangent to which at $A$ and $B$ intersect on the line $x+2 y+5=0$.Question: The area of an equilateral triangle inscribed in S , is
questions.solution:
(i) We have $\left(x^2+y^2-2 x-8\right)-2 \lambda y=0$, which is the form of $S+\lambda L=0$ $\therefore$ On solving $S=0$ and $L=0$ where $S \equiv x^2+y^2-2 x-8=0$ and $L \equiv y=0$ Put $y=0 \Rightarrow x^2-2 x-8=0(x-4)(x+2)=0 \quad x=-2$ or 4 $\therefore \quad A \equiv(4,0) ; B \equiv(-2,0)$ $\Rightarrow$ Distance between $A$ and $B=6$ (ii) Equation of $A B$ which is chord of contact with respect to $P\left(x_1, y_1\right)$ is $\mathrm{xx}_1+\mathrm{yy}_1-1\left(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x}_1\right)-\lambda\left(\mathrm{y}+\mathrm{y}_1\right)-8=0$ $\Rightarrow x\left(x_1-1\right)+y\left(y_1-\lambda\right)-\left(x_1+\lambda y_1+8\right)=0$ $\ldots(1)$
Also equation of $A B$ is $x$-axis i.e $0 \cdot x+1 \cdot y+0 \cdot c=0$ $\ldots(2)$ $\therefore$ On comparing (1) \& (2), we get $\frac{\mathrm{x}_1-1}{0}=\frac{\mathrm{y}_1-\lambda}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{x}_1+\lambda \mathrm{y}_1+8}{0}=k \text { say }$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}_1-1=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{x}_1=1 ; \mathrm{y}_1=\lambda+\mathrm{k}\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { and } x_1+\lambda y_1+8=0 \\ & \Rightarrow 9+\lambda y_1=0\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{y}_1=-\frac{9}{\lambda}$ Also $\mathrm{x}_1+2 \mathrm{y}_1+5=0 \Rightarrow 1-\frac{18}{\lambda}+5=0$ $\Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{18}{6} \Rightarrow \lambda=3$ $\therefore \quad$ Required equation of circle $S=0$ is $x^2+y^2-2 x-6 y-8=0$ Now radius of circle $S=0$ is $r=\sqrt{1+9+8}=3 \sqrt{2}$ Hence area of equilateral triangle inscribed in a circle of radius ' $r$ ' $=\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{4} r^2=\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{4} \cdot 18$ $=\frac{27 \sqrt{3}}{2}$ (iii) If the circle $x^2+y^2-10 x+2 y+c=0$ is orthogonal to $S \equiv x^2+y^2-2 x-6 y-8=0$, so by applying condition of orthogonality, we get $2[(-5)(-1)+(1)(-3)]=c-8 \Rightarrow 4=c-8$ Hence $c=12$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the family of circles $x^2+y^2-2 x-2 \lambda y-8=0$ passing through two fixed points $A$ and $B$. Also $S=0$ is a circle of this family, the tangent to which at $A$ and $B$ intersect on the line $x+2 y+5=0$.Question: If the circle $x^2+y^2-10 x+2 y+c=0$ is orthogonal to $S=0$, then the value of $c$ equals
questions.solution:
(i) We have $\left(x^2+y^2-2 x-8\right)-2 \lambda y=0$, which is the form of $S+\lambda L=0$ $\therefore$ On solving $S=0$ and $L=0$ where $S \equiv x^2+y^2-2 x-8=0$ and $L \equiv y=0$ Put $y=0 \Rightarrow x^2-2 x-8=0(x-4)(x+2)=0 \quad x=-2$ or 4 $\therefore \quad A \equiv(4,0) ; B \equiv(-2,0)$ $\Rightarrow$ Distance between $A$ and $B=6$ (ii) Equation of $A B$ which is chord of contact with respect to $P\left(x_1, y_1\right)$ is $\mathrm{xx}_1+\mathrm{yy}_1-1\left(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{x}_1\right)-\lambda\left(\mathrm{y}+\mathrm{y}_1\right)-8=0$ $\Rightarrow x\left(x_1-1\right)+y\left(y_1-\lambda\right)-\left(x_1+\lambda y_1+8\right)=0$ $\ldots(1)$
Also equation of $A B$ is $x$-axis i.e $0 \cdot x+1 \cdot y+0 \cdot c=0$ $\ldots(2)$ $\therefore$ On comparing (1) \& (2), we get $\frac{\mathrm{x}_1-1}{0}=\frac{\mathrm{y}_1-\lambda}{1}=\frac{\mathrm{x}_1+\lambda \mathrm{y}_1+8}{0}=k \text { say }$ $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}_1-1=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{x}_1=1 ; \mathrm{y}_1=\lambda+\mathrm{k}\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { and } x_1+\lambda y_1+8=0 \\ & \Rightarrow 9+\lambda y_1=0\end{aligned}$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{y}_1=-\frac{9}{\lambda}$ Also $\mathrm{x}_1+2 \mathrm{y}_1+5=0 \Rightarrow 1-\frac{18}{\lambda}+5=0$ $\Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{18}{6} \Rightarrow \lambda=3$ $\therefore \quad$ Required equation of circle $S=0$ is $x^2+y^2-2 x-6 y-8=0$ Now radius of circle $S=0$ is $r=\sqrt{1+9+8}=3 \sqrt{2}$ Hence area of equilateral triangle inscribed in a circle of radius ' $r$ ' $=\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{4} r^2=\frac{3 \sqrt{3}}{4} \cdot 18$ $=\frac{27 \sqrt{3}}{2}$ (iii) If the circle $x^2+y^2-10 x+2 y+c=0$ is orthogonal to $S \equiv x^2+y^2-2 x-6 y-8=0$, so by applying condition of orthogonality, we get $2[(-5)(-1)+(1)(-3)]=c-8 \Rightarrow 4=c-8$ Hence $c=12$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider two circles $\mathrm{S}_1: \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-9=0$ and $\mathrm{S}_2: \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-10 \mathrm{x}+9=0$.Question: Length of the common chord of $S_1=0$ and $S_2=0$ is
questions.solution:
$\left.x^2+y^2-10 x+9=0\right\}$ circles are orthogonal $x^2+y^2-9=0$ (i) $\frac{1}{2}(3)(4)=\frac{1}{2} 51$ (Area of $\triangle \mathrm{POC}$ written in two ways) $I=\frac{12}{5}$
Length of common chord $=2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{24}{5}$ (ii) $\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{3}{a}=\frac{4}{5+a} \Rightarrow 15+3 a=4 a \Rightarrow a=15$ Hence $P(-15,0)$ Now $\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{3}{15}=\frac{1}{5}$ $\cos \theta=1-2 \sin ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}=1-(2) \frac{1}{25}=\frac{23}{25}$ Ans. (iii) $\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{5} \Rightarrow \tan \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{24}}=m_{C T}=$ slope of their common tangent $\therefore \quad$ Equation of common tangent is $(y-0)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{24}}(x+15)$ $\Rightarrow x-\sqrt{24} y+15=0$ $\therefore \quad$ Distance from $(9,0)$ is $\left|\frac{9+15}{5}\right|=\frac{24}{5}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider two circles $\mathrm{S}_1: \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-9=0$ and $\mathrm{S}_2: \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-10 \mathrm{x}+9=0$.Question: If $\theta$ is the angle between the two common tangents of $S_1=0$ and $S_2=0$, then $\cos \theta$ equals
questions.solution:
$\left.x^2+y^2-10 x+9=0\right\}$ circles are orthogonal $x^2+y^2-9=0$ (i) $\frac{1}{2}(3)(4)=\frac{1}{2} 51$ (Area of $\triangle \mathrm{POC}$ written in two ways) $I=\frac{12}{5}$
Length of common chord $=2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{24}{5}$ (ii) $\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{3}{a}=\frac{4}{5+a} \Rightarrow 15+3 a=4 a \Rightarrow a=15$ Hence $P(-15,0)$ Now $\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{3}{15}=\frac{1}{5}$ $\cos \theta=1-2 \sin ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}=1-(2) \frac{1}{25}=\frac{23}{25}$ Ans. (iii) $\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{5} \Rightarrow \tan \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{24}}=m_{C T}=$ slope of their common tangent $\therefore \quad$ Equation of common tangent is $(y-0)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{24}}(x+15)$ $\Rightarrow x-\sqrt{24} y+15=0$ $\therefore \quad$ Distance from $(9,0)$ is $\left|\frac{9+15}{5}\right|=\frac{24}{5}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Consider two circles $\mathrm{S}_1: \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-9=0$ and $\mathrm{S}_2: \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{y}^2-10 \mathrm{x}+9=0$.Question: Distance of a common tangent of $S_1=0$ and $S_2=0$ from point $(9,0)$ is
questions.solution:
$\left.x^2+y^2-10 x+9=0\right\}$ circles are orthogonal $x^2+y^2-9=0$ (i) $\frac{1}{2}(3)(4)=\frac{1}{2} 51$ (Area of $\triangle \mathrm{POC}$ written in two ways) $I=\frac{12}{5}$
Length of common chord $=2 \mathrm{I}=\frac{24}{5}$ (ii) $\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{3}{a}=\frac{4}{5+a} \Rightarrow 15+3 a=4 a \Rightarrow a=15$ Hence $P(-15,0)$ Now $\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{3}{15}=\frac{1}{5}$ $\cos \theta=1-2 \sin ^2 \frac{\theta}{2}=1-(2) \frac{1}{25}=\frac{23}{25}$ Ans. (iii) $\sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{5} \Rightarrow \tan \frac{\theta}{2}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{24}}=m_{C T}=$ slope of their common tangent $\therefore \quad$ Equation of common tangent is $(y-0)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{24}}(x+15)$ $\Rightarrow x-\sqrt{24} y+15=0$ $\therefore \quad$ Distance from $(9,0)$ is $\left|\frac{9+15}{5}\right|=\frac{24}{5}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider a circle $S: x^2+y^2-6 x-4 y-3=0$ with centre $C$ and $P$ be the point $(-1,-1)$. Also $P A$ and $P B$ are tangent drawn to the circle $S$.Question: The area of the quadrilateral $P A C B$ is equal to:
questions.solution: (i) Area of quadrilateral $=r L=4 \times 3=12$ (ii) Circle circumscribing the triangle $P A B$ is the circle described on $C P$ as diameter $$ \therefore \quad \text { radius }=\frac{5}{2} $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider a circle $S: x^2+y^2-6 x-4 y-3=0$ with centre $C$ and $P$ be the point $(-1,-1)$. Also $P A$ and $P B$ are tangent drawn to the circle $S$.Question: Radius of the circle circumscribing the triangle $P A B$ is:
questions.solution: (i) Area of quadrilateral $=r L=4 \times 3=12$ (ii) Circle circumscribing the triangle $P A B$ is the circle described on $C P$ as diameter $$ \therefore \quad \text { radius }=\frac{5}{2} $$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $P A B$ be a triangle where $A(1,1), B(3,3)$ and $P$ be a variable point such that $P A^2+P B^2=6$. The locus of point $P$ is $S=0$. From point $Q(3,7)$, pair of tangents are drawn to the curve $S=0$ which touches the curve $S=0$ at $C$ and $D$. Let $S_1=0$ be the circumcircle of $\triangle Q C D$. Then:Question: Equation of common chord of $S=0$ and $S_1=0$ is:
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& S=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad(x-2)^2+(y-2)^2=1 \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-4 x-4 y+7=0 \\
& r=L_{\text {medium }}=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{2 P A^2+2 P B^2-A B^2}=\frac{\sqrt{12-8}}{2}=1 \\
& S_1=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad(x-3)(x-2)+(y-7)(y-2)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-5 x-9 y+20=0 \\
& \text { Common chord } \Rightarrow x+5 y-13=0 \\
& \cos \theta=\frac{r_1^2+r_2^2-d^2}{2 r_1 r_2}=\frac{1+\frac{13}{2}-\frac{13}{2}}{2 \cdot 1 \times \sqrt{\frac{13}{2}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{26}} \quad\left(r_1=1, r_2=\sqrt{\frac{13}{2}}=d\right) \\
& \tan \theta=5 \text {. } \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $P A B$ be a triangle where $A(1,1), B(3,3)$ and $P$ be a variable point such that $P A^2+P B^2=6$. The locus of point $P$ is $S=0$. From point $Q(3,7)$, pair of tangents are drawn to the curve $S=0$ which touches the curve $S=0$ at $C$ and $D$. Let $S_1=0$ be the circumcircle of $\triangle Q C D$. Then:Question: If $\theta$ is the acute angle between $S=0$ and $S_1=0$, then $\tan \theta$ equals:
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& S=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad(x-2)^2+(y-2)^2=1 \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-4 x-4 y+7=0 \\
& r=L_{\text {medium }}=\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{2 P A^2+2 P B^2-A B^2}=\frac{\sqrt{12-8}}{2}=1 \\
& S_1=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad(x-3)(x-2)+(y-7)(y-2)=0 \\
& \Rightarrow x^2+y^2-5 x-9 y+20=0 \\
& \text { Common chord } \Rightarrow x+5 y-13=0 \\
& \cos \theta=\frac{r_1^2+r_2^2-d^2}{2 r_1 r_2}=\frac{1+\frac{13}{2}-\frac{13}{2}}{2 \cdot 1 \times \sqrt{\frac{13}{2}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{26}} \quad\left(r_1=1, r_2=\sqrt{\frac{13}{2}}=d\right) \\
& \tan \theta=5 \text {. } \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A ray of light strikes a line mirror $y=0$ along the line $L_1: x-3 y=1$ and reflects along the line $L_2=0$. There is another line mirror $x-4=0$ in the way of reflected ray. After 2nd reflection ray moves along the line $\mathrm{L}_3=0$ and finally it travels in the direction of the line $\mathrm{L}_4=0$ after 3rd reflection from line mirror $y+2=0$. On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions:Question: Equation of the circle inscribed in the closed figure is -
questions.solution: Centre of the circle is $(1,-1)$ and $r=\left|\frac{1-3-1}{\sqrt{1+9}}\right|=\frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}$ $\therefore$ equation of circle is $(x-1)^2+(y+1)^2=\frac{9}{10}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A ray of light strikes a line mirror $y=0$ along the line $L_1: x-3 y=1$ and reflects along the line $L_2=0$. There is another line mirror $x-4=0$ in the way of reflected ray. After 2nd reflection ray moves along the line $\mathrm{L}_3=0$ and finally it travels in the direction of the line $\mathrm{L}_4=0$ after 3rd reflection from line mirror $y+2=0$. On the basis of above informations, answer the following questions:Question: Equation of the line $\mathrm{L}_4=0$ is -
questions.solution: After $I^{\text {st }}$ reflection, equation of $L_2=0$ is Slope $=-1 / 3$ and point $(1,0)$ $y-0=-\frac{1}{3}(x-1) \quad \Rightarrow \quad x+3 y=1$ point of incidence on the mirror $x-4=0$ is $(4,-1)$. Equation of $L_3=0$ is $y+1=\frac{1}{3}(x-4)$ $x-3 y=7$ again point of incidence on the line mirror $\mathrm{y}+2=0 \text { is }(1,-2)$ equation of $L_4=0$ is $\begin{aligned} & y+2=-\frac{1}{3}(x-1) \\ & x+3 y+5=0\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
